description: Experience the iconic taste of Hong Kong with these delightful Pineapple Buns, known as Bo Lo Bao. Each bun features a soft, sweet bread base crowned with a crispy, crumbly, and slightly sweet topping that resembles a pineapple's skin. Perfect for breakfast or a snack, these buns offer a comforting blend of textures and flavors that will transport you straight to a bustling Cantonese bakery.

instructions.title: Prepare the Bun Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine 250g flour, 30g sugar, and 5g instant dry yeast. Gradually add 120ml warm milk and 1 beaten egg, mixing until a shaggy dough forms. Knead on a lightly floured surface for 5 minutes, then incorporate 30g softened butter. Continue kneading for another 10-15 minutes until the dough is smooth and elastic. Place in a greased bowl, cover, and let it proof in a warm place for 60-90 minutes, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Make the Pineapple Topping
instructions.text: While the dough is proofing, prepare the topping. In a separate bowl, cream together 60g softened butter and 50g powdered sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in 1 egg yolk and 1 tablespoon evaporated milk until well combined. Gradually add 100g all-purpose flour, mixing until a soft, crumbly dough forms. Do not overmix. Cover and refrigerate for 20 minutes to firm up.

instructions.title: Shape the Buns
instructions.text: Once the bun dough has doubled, gently punch it down and divide it into 8 equal portions. Roll each portion into a smooth ball. Place the dough balls on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between them.

instructions.title: Apply the Topping
instructions.text: Remove the topping dough from the fridge. Divide it into 8 equal portions. On a lightly floured surface or between two pieces of parchment paper, flatten each topping portion into a thin disc, large enough to cover the top of each bun. Gently place a topping disc over each dough ball, pressing lightly to adhere. You can score a crisscross pattern on the topping with a knife for the classic pineapple look.

instructions.title: Second Proof and Preheat Oven
instructions.text: Cover the shaped buns loosely with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let them proof again in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy. Towards the end of the second proof, preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F).

instructions.title: Bake the Pineapple Buns
instructions.text: Just before baking, gently brush the egg wash (1 egg yolk mixed with 1 tsp water) over the surface of the pineapple topping. Bake in the preheated oven for 18-22 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and the buns are cooked through. The internal temperature should be around 90°C (195°F).

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, remove the buns from the oven and transfer them to a wire rack to cool slightly. Pineapple buns are best enjoyed warm, fresh from the oven, perhaps with a slice of butter inside.

tags: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe
tips: Ensure your yeast is active by dissolving it in warm milk with a pinch of sugar first. If it doesnt foam after 5-10 minutes, your yeast may be old.
For an extra fluffy bun, use bread flour instead of all-purpose flour for the dough. This increases gluten development.
To achieve a perfectly crisp topping, make sure your oven is fully preheated and avoid opening the oven door too frequently during baking.
article:
Craving that iconic sweet and savory aroma that wafts from Hong Kong bakeries? You're not alone. The Pineapple Bun, or Bo Lo Bao, is a beloved staple of Chinese baking, known for its perfectly contrasting textures: a crumbly, sugary, crackly crust atop a soft, fluffy, slightly sweet bread bun. While its name suggests a tropical fruit, there's no pineapple in sight; the moniker comes from the bun's distinctive, scored topping that resembles a pineapple's skin.

Making these at home might seem like a daunting task, but with the right guidance, you can replicate this culinary delight in your own kitchen. Forget the long queues at your local cha chaan teng; this comprehensive guide will walk you through the ultimate Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe, ensuring you achieve bakery-quality results every single time. Get ready to impress your friends and family with the irresistible charm of homemade Bo Lo Bao.

## What is Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao?

Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is more than just a pastry; it's a cultural icon, especially in Hong Kong. Its charm lies in the beautiful dichotomy of its two main components. The "pineapple" part refers to the golden-brown, sugary crust that tops the bun. This crust is typically made from a mixture of sugar, butter, flour, and egg, which bakes into a crisp, crumbly layer that shatters with each bite. Beneath this delightful shell lies a soft, slightly sweet yeast bread, similar in texture to a milk bread, light and airy.

Originating from Hong Kong, Bo Lo Bao is a quintessential item found in bakeries and cha chaan tengs (Hong Kong-style cafes) across the globe. It’s a popular breakfast item, an afternoon snack, or simply a comforting treat enjoyed any time of day. While the traditional bun is plain inside, variations with fillings like sweet custard, red bean paste, or even savory char siu (BBQ pork) have gained popularity. The most classic way to enjoy it, however, is warmed with a thick slice of butter tucked inside – a mouth-watering combination known as Bo Lo Yau. Mastering your own Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe allows you to customize this classic to your exact preference.

## Ingredients You Need

Achieving the perfect Pineapple Bun requires attention to both the bread dough and the signature crumb topping. Gathering the right ingredients is the first step towards a successful Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe.

**For the Bun Dough:**
*   **All-purpose flour:** Or bread flour for a chewier texture.
*   **Granulated sugar:** For sweetness and to activate yeast.
*   **Instant dry yeast:** Ensures a fluffy rise.
*   **Salt:** Balances the flavors.
*   **Milk:** Whole milk works best for richness.
*   **Egg:** Adds richness and helps with texture.
*   **Unsalted butter:** Softened, for tenderness and flavor.

**For the Crumb Topping:**
*   **All-purpose flour:** The base for the crumb.
*   **Granulated sugar:** For sweetness and crispness.
*   **Unsalted butter:** Chilled and cubed, crucial for the crumbly texture.
*   **Egg yolk:** Adds richness and color.
*   **Milk powder (optional):** Enhances flavor and aroma.
*   **Baking powder (optional):** For a slightly lighter, more tender crumb.

**For Assembly:**
*   **Egg wash:** 1 egg yolk whisked with 1 tbsp milk, for brushing the topping before baking to achieve a golden sheen.

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Embarking on this Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe is a rewarding journey. Follow these steps for best results:

**1. Prepare the Bun Dough:**
    *   In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, yeast, and salt.
    *   In a separate bowl, whisk warm milk and egg.
    *   Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
    *   Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 5-7 minutes until smooth and elastic. If using a stand mixer, knead with a dough hook for 5 minutes.
    *   Gradually add the softened butter, kneading until fully incorporated and the dough is soft, smooth, and passes the windowpane test (stretches thin enough to see through without tearing).
    *   Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it proof in a warm place for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

**2. Make the Crumb Topping:**
    *   In a bowl, cream together the chilled butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
    *   Beat in the egg yolk until fully combined.
    *   Gradually add the flour (and milk powder/baking powder if using), mixing until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
    *   Bring the crumbs together to form a soft, pliable dough. Do not overmix. Wrap it in plastic wrap and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.

**3. Shape the Buns:**
    *   Once the dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release air.
    *   Divide the dough into 8-10 equal portions (about 60-70g each).
    *   Shape each portion into a smooth ball. Place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between each.

**4. Apply the Topping:**
    *   Take the chilled crumb topping dough and divide it into the same number of portions as your buns.
    *   Flatten each portion of topping dough into a thin disc (about 3-4 inches in diameter). You can do this between two pieces of parchment paper or by hand.
    *   Carefully place a topping disc over each bun, gently pressing it down to adhere. Score the topping with a knife in a crosshatch pattern, resembling a pineapple.

**5. Second Proofing and Baking:**
    *   Cover the baking sheet loosely with plastic wrap or a clean kitchen towel and let the buns proof for another 30-45 minutes in a warm place, or until visibly puffy.
    *   Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C).
    *   Just before baking, gently brush the tops of the buns with the egg wash.
    *   Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the buns are golden brown and the topping is crisp and caramelized.
    *   Remove from the oven and transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect bakery-style Pineapple Bun requires a few insider tricks. Mastering these tips will elevate your Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe from good to extraordinary.

*   **Don't Rush the Proofing:** Patience is key. Allow your dough to proof fully in a warm, draft-free environment. Under-proofed dough results in dense buns, while over-proofed dough can lead to a yeasty flavor and collapse.
*   **Perfecting the Crumb Topping:** The butter for the topping should be chilled, but not rock hard. This helps create that desirable crumbly texture. When flattening the topping, aim for a thickness that will crackle nicely without being so thick it falls off during baking. A thin, even layer is ideal.
*   **The Right Dough Consistency:** The bun dough should be soft and slightly sticky after kneading. Resist the urge to add too much extra flour, as this will result in dry, tough buns. A supple, elastic dough is what you're aiming for.
*   **Scoring is Key:** The scoring on the topping isn't just for aesthetics; it helps the topping expand and crackle properly, giving it that characteristic "pineapple" look. Use a sharp knife and make shallow cuts.
*   **Hot Oven for a Quick Rise:** Baking at a relatively high temperature helps the buns rise quickly and sets the crumb topping, creating that beautiful, crisp shell.
*   **Cooling Properly:** Always cool your baked buns on a wire rack. This prevents the bottoms from becoming soggy due to trapped steam, ensuring a crisp crust and soft interior. Following a well-tested Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe is crucial, but these tips will guarantee success.

## Serving Suggestions

The beauty of Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao lies in its versatility. While delicious on its own, here are some classic and creative serving suggestions:

*   **The Classic Bo Lo Yau:** The quintessential way to enjoy a Pineapple Bun is warm, sliced horizontally, and generously filled with a thick slab of cold butter. The butter melts into the warm bun and crust, creating an irresistible sweet and savory delight.
*   **Breakfast or Tea Time:** Serve them alongside a cup of Hong Kong-style milk tea, coffee, or hot lemon tea for an authentic cha chaan teng experience.
*   **With Custard:** Split and fill with a dollop of homemade or store-bought sweet custard for an extra layer of indulgence.
*   **Plain and Simple:** Sometimes, the best way to enjoy a freshly baked Pineapple Bun is just as it is, allowing the contrasting textures and subtle sweetness to shine.

## Variations and Substitutions

This Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe is a fantastic starting point, but feel free to get creative and customize it to your liking.

*   **Filled Buns:** Experiment with fillings! Beyond the classic butter, consider sweet options like red bean paste, coconut cream, taro paste, or even a simple vanilla custard. For a savory twist, try a small portion of char siu (BBQ pork) filling.
*   **Flour Types:** While all-purpose flour works well, using bread flour for the bun dough will yield a chewier texture. For an even softer bun, you can incorporate a small amount of cake flour.
*   **Dairy-Free Options:** For those with dietary restrictions, plant-based milk and vegan butter can be substituted in both the bun and the topping. The texture might vary slightly, but the essence of the bun will remain.
*   **Less Sweet:** If you prefer a less sweet pastry, you can slightly reduce the amount of sugar in both the bun dough and the crumb topping.
*   **Additions to Topping:** A pinch of cinnamon or vanilla extract can add another dimension of flavor to the crumb topping.
Don't be afraid to put your own spin on this versatile Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe.

In conclusion, the journey of making Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao at home is incredibly rewarding. From the satisfying process of kneading the dough to the aroma that fills your kitchen as they bake, and finally, that first glorious bite of crisp topping and fluffy bun – it's an experience that truly transports you. This comprehensive Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe provides you with all the tools and knowledge to recreate this beloved Chinese bakery classic. So, roll up your sleeves, gather your ingredients, and get ready to enjoy the ultimate homemade Bo Lo Bao. Happy baking!
